title: acaena × langei-axelii
intro: Acaena × langei-axelii, also known as New Zealand thyme, is a hybrid flowering plant within the ruscus family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can be attributed to its essential oils. Its health benefits include treating minor wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues, as well as providing relief from fever and respiratory problems. Acaena × langei-axelii is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal purposes, although its availability and usage may vary due to its regional distribution.
